stem: A candidate enters the hiring pipeline. The probability of passing resume screening is 77%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing phone interview is 84%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing onsite interview is 98%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing offer acceptance is between 66% and 82% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the candidate is hired?

ground_truth.answer: P(hired) ranges from 0.418 to 0.52 as the offer acceptance pass rate varies from 66% to 82%. Point estimate: 0.469.

solution_steps: The candidate must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the offer acceptance stage creates a 10.2 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the offer acceptance rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
